rag_rules: Generate AGREE annex blocks inside component implementation scope for signal flow requirements
Use assign statements to bind outputs to expressions that reference visible inputs and subcomponent paths
Avoid using AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions; use model-provided ports or constants instead
Ensure all identifiers in AGREE expressions match exactly the case of names in the model index
generic_rules: Place AGREE contracts in the appropriate annex scope (type or implementation) based on the requirement context
Use visible identifiers from the model index to maintain consistency and avoid unresolved references
Avoid mixing component type and implementation contracts; keep them separate in their respective annex blocks
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use assign statements for implementation-level bindings in component implementation annexes
Use exact case names from the model index in AGREE expressions to avoid unresolved references
Place AGREE annex blocks within the owning component declaration to maintain proper scope
anti_patterns: Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions; use model-provided ports or constants instead
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assign statements instead
Do not invent or copy identifiers from examples; use only those visible in the current component scope
